last_item: Mode-S discovery/convergence pass (backlog actionable-empty after iter 21). 5-agent parallel panel, same coverage as iters 15-21 (Dashboard+Query; Roles+Backups+BackupStorageForm; Alerts+Migrate; Settings+Tuning+Pooler+Login; nav/IA+first-run+consistency), deliberately HIGH bar, given the full shipped/rejected digest.
last_result: CONVERGED — no new high/med item; first stable_streak increment 0 -> 1/3. Three views returned clean 'no new high/med' (Dashboard+Query mature honest-state; Alerts+Migrate — verified the 3-stage overwrite gate + that reset() clears confirm; Settings/Tuning/Pooler/Login — verified lockout recovery, the DatabaseTuning reassurance Callouts, the Pooler repoint copy). Two candidates surfaced and BOTH collapsed on code-level inspection (self-rejected, no panel — iter-5/13/14 precedent): (1) Migrate overwrite confirm 'add dismissible={false}' — FALSE PREMISE: the destructive action fires ONLY via the 'Overwrite & migrate' button (disabled until the typed name matches); dismissing (Escape/backdrop/Back) calls setConfirmOpen(false) and executes nothing, so dismissing is the SAFE escape — mirror opposite of iter-20's SecretsModal (dismiss=irreversible loss). Non-dismissible here would TRAP a user inside a destructive dialog — negative payoff. (2) BackupStorageForm 'clear saved credential' — speculative + backend-dependent (agent couldn't confirm the API accepts a clear signal); changing a credential already works; out of scope for this frontend-only loop. Docs-only iteration (backlog/learnings/progress/state), no code shipped, no hard gates needed. LESSON: before locking down a dialog's dismiss paths, ask WHICH DIRECTION is dangerous — dismissible={false} is right only when dismissing itself causes irreversible loss (SecretsModal); when dismissing CANCELS a destructive action (every type-to-confirm execute-gate), the casual escape is a feature, keep it. Don't copy iter-20's pattern onto a confirm dialog whose gate is on the execute button. Backlog still actionable-empty (NEEDS-BACKEND badge item + 2 low/watch nits). Next iteration: run another Mode-S convergence check; two more clean passes -> write COMPLETE.md and stop the loop.
notes: PURE UX-IMPROVEMENT LOOP. Sibling of the COMPLETED scripts/ralph/ shadcn-migration track. Capability parity is sacred (Vitest suite is the contract); layout/flow/copy are free to change. Restraint over churn -- rejecting a change and recording why is a normal outcome. Frontend/UX only, never touch Go. go build is blocked in-sandbox (snap-confine); run with sandbox disabled. See PROMPT.md + UX-PRINCIPLES.md + PERSONAS.md + UI-RULES.md.
north_star: The indiepg panel is the easiest, clearest, safest way for an indie hacker to run their Postgres -- minimal surface, co-located workflows, honest state, no over-design. Stop when the UX has converged (stable_streak >= 3).
